confirmed.title: php-overview.md claims 9 column / 6 filter types; actual is 12 / 8 (EN)
confirmed.suggested_fix: Change '9 column types, 6 filter types' to '12 column types, 8 filter types'.
confirmed.detail: The Packages table row for arqel-dev/table reads 'Table builder, 9 column types, 6 filter types, TableQueryBuilder'. On disk packages/table/src/Columns/ has 12 concrete column classes (Badge, Boolean, Computed, Date, Icon, Image, Number, Relationship, Select, Text, TextInput, Toggle) and packages/table/src/Filters/ has 8 concrete filter classes (DateRange, MultiSelect, QueryBuilder, Scope, Select, Ternary, Text, Trashed). Round 1 already corrected this exact pair in apps/docs/reference/php/table.md (to 12/8) but php-overview.md was not updated.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides on disk. apps/docs/reference/php-overview.md:21 reads "arqel-dev/table | Table builder, 9 column types, 6 filter types, TableQueryBuilder". On disk, packages/table/src/Columns/ contains 12 concrete column classes (Badge, Boolean, Computed, Date, Icon, Image, Number, Relationship, Select, Text, TextInput, Toggle) and packages/table/src/Filters/ contains 8 concrete filter classes (DateRange, MultiSelect, QueryBuilder, Scope, Select, Ternary, Text, Trashed; the Constraints subdir is not a filter type). So the doc's "9/6" is objectively false vs the real "12/8". The doc is in-scope (apps/docs, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated). The sibling page apps/docs/reference/php/table.md was already corrected to "12 column types, 8 filter types", confirming the intended counts and that php-overview.md was simply missed. The ledger (reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json) has zero php-overview entries, so this is not a duplicate.

confirmed.title: fields.md claims ValidationBridge has 19 built-in translators; actual is 21 (EN)
confirmed.suggested_fix: Change '19 built-in translators' to '21 built-in translators'.
confirmed.detail: Doc: 'Translates Laravel rule arrays to a Zod schema. 19 built-in translators.' packages/fields/src/ValidationBridge.php registers 21 distinct default rules via self::$rules['...']: string, numeric, integer, boolean, array, date, file, image, email, url, uuid, min, max, size, regex, in, not_in, unique, nullable, required, mimetypes. (21 distinct keys, no external registration sites.)
confirmed.why_real: Verified both sides. apps/docs/reference/php/fields.md:75 states ValidationBridge has "19 built-in translators." The source packages/fields/src/ValidationBridge.php registers 21 distinct default rule keys via self::$rules['...'] in registerDefaults: string, numeric, integer, boolean, array, date, file, image, email, url, uuid, min, max, size, regex, in, not_in, unique, nullable, required, mimetypes (grep -oE | sort -u count = 21). No external registration sites for defaults. The doc number (19) objectively contradicts the code (21). In-scope (apps/docs, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated), checkable, and not present in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json (ledger covers table.md counts and other items, not this fields.md translator count).

I counted them via grep of distinct self::$rules['...'] keys = 21. So '19' is objectively false (off by 2). confirmed.title: advanced/*.md 'Related' links to packages/*/SKILL.md use wrong relative depth (../../ → resolves to apps/packages, must be ../../../)
confirmed.suggested_fix: Change ../../packages/... to ../../../packages/... (and ../../PLANNING/... to ../../../PLANNING/...) in the Related section of all 6 advanced pages across EN, pt-BR, and es.
confirmed.detail: From apps/docs/advanced/command-palette.md, the link `[packages/core/SKILL.md](../../packages/core/SKILL.md)` resolves to apps/docs/advanced/../../packages → /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/apps/packages/core/SKILL.md, which does NOT exist (verified with realpath -m + ls). The real target packages/core/SKILL.md is one level higher, requiring ../../../. The page is 3 dirs deep (apps/docs/advanced) so it needs three ../. This same wrong-depth pattern affects the 'Related' SKILL.md link on all 6 advanced pages — command-palette→core, custom-fields→fields-advanced, dashboards→widgets, mcp→mcp, multi-tenancy→tenant, tables-v2→table — and is mirrored identically under apps/docs/pt-BR/advanced/ and apps/docs/es/advanced/ (18 files total). The accompanying PLANNING/* links on those same lines (e.g. ../../PLANNING/09-fase-2-essenciais.md → apps/PLANNING/...) are broken by the identical depth error.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides on disk. apps/docs/advanced/command-palette.md:161 contains the link [`packages/core/SKILL.md`](../../packages/core/SKILL.md). From apps/docs/advanced/, ../../ climbs only to apps/, so the link resolves to apps/packages/core/SKILL.md, which does NOT exist (verified: `ls` errors, `realpath -m` confirms target apps/packages/core/SKILL.md). The real file is packages/core/SKILL.md (exists, 37821 bytes), three levels up, requiring ../../../. The page is 3 dirs deep (apps/docs/advanced), so it needs three ../. This is an objectively broken relative link, not a wording nit. Pattern verified systemic across all 6 EN advanced pages' Related SKILL.md lines and mirrored in pt-BR and es (18 files); the adjacent PLANNING/* links (../../PLANNING/...) are broken by the same depth error (resolve to apps/PLANNING/... which does not exist). confirmed.title: php-overview.md states table has '9 column types, 6 filter types' but disk has 12 columns and 8 filters
confirmed.suggested_fix: Update the table row in apps/docs/reference/php-overview.md to '12 column types, 8 filter types' to match disk (and the already-corrected table.md).
confirmed.detail: Note: actual path is apps/docs/reference/php-overview.md. The packages table row for arqel-dev/table reads 'Table builder, 9 column types, 6 filter types, TableQueryBuilder'. On disk packages/table/src/Columns/ contains 12 *Column.php classes (Badge, Boolean, Computed, Date, Icon, Image, Number, Relationship, Select, Text, TextInput, Toggle) and packages/table/src/Filters/ contains 8 *Filter.php classes (DateRange, MultiSelect, QueryBuilder, Scope, Select, Ternary, Text, Trashed). Round 1 fixed this exact stale count in apps/docs/reference/php/table.md but the duplicated claim in php-overview.md (a different in-scope file) was left stale.
confirmed.why_real: Verified both sides on disk. The doc file exists at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/apps/docs/reference/php-overview.md (the finding's cited path with /php/ was wrong, but it self-corrected to the real path). Line 21 reads: "| `arqel-dev/table` | Table builder, 9 column types, 6 filter types, TableQueryBuilder |". Reality: packages/table/src/Columns/ contains 12 *Column.php classes (Badge, Boolean, Computed, Date, Icon, Image, Number, Relationship, Select, Text, TextInput, Toggle) and packages/table/src/Filters/ contains 8 *Filter.php classes (DateRange, MultiSelect, QueryBuilder, Scope, Select, Ternary, Text, Trashed; the Constraints dir is not a filter file). So "9 column types" and "6 filter types" are both objectively false. The doc is in-scope (apps/docs, not PLANNING/tickets/superpowers/CHANGELOG/generated), the claim is concrete and checkable, and it contradicts current code. Ledger check: the only related entries are for a DIFFERENT file (apps/docs/reference/php/table.md:3) fixed in Round 1; php-overview.md is not present in the ledger, so this duplicated stale claim is a distinct unfixed instance.

confirmed.title: php-overview.md table row claims '9 column types, 6 filter types' but disk has 12 and 8
confirmed.suggested_fix: Change to '12 column types, 8 filter types' in all three php-overview.md files (EN line 21, pt-BR line 21, es line 21).
confirmed.detail: The Packages table row for `arqel-dev/table` reads 'Table builder, 9 column types, 6 filter types, TableQueryBuilder'. On disk packages/table/src/Columns/ has 12 column classes (Badge, Boolean, Computed, Date, Icon, Image, Number, Relationship, Select, Text, TextInput, Toggle) and packages/table/src/Filters/ has 8 filter classes (DateRange, MultiSelect, QueryBuilder, Scope, Select, Ternary, Text, Trashed). This is the same stale '9/6' that Round 1 fixed in reference/php/table.md, but php-overview.md is a separate file that still carries it. Mirrors also affected: apps/docs/pt-BR/reference/php-overview.md:21 ('9 column types, 6 filter types') and apps/docs/es/reference/php-overview.md:21 ('9 tipos de columna, 6 tipos de filtro').
confirmed.why_real: Verified both sides. apps/docs/reference/php-overview.md:21 states the arqel-dev/table row as 'Table builder, 9 column types, 6 filter types, TableQueryBuilder'. On disk, packages/table/src/Columns/ contains 12 column classes (BadgeColumn, BooleanColumn, ComputedColumn, DateColumn, IconColumn, ImageColumn, NumberColumn, RelationshipColumn, SelectColumn, TextColumn, TextInputColumn, ToggleColumn) and packages/table/src/Filters/ contains 8 filter classes (DateRange, MultiSelect, QueryBuilder, Scope, Select, Ternary, Text, Trashed). So 9!=12 and 6!=8 — the claim is objectively false. The doc is in-scope (apps/docs, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated). Not in the ledger: grep for 'php-overview' returns 0; the existing 9/6 ledger entries are for the distinct file apps/docs/reference/php/table.md (Round 1), not php-overview.md.

confirmed.title: typescript-overview.md / typescript/react.md state Inertia peer '@inertiajs/react 2+' but packages require ^3.0.0
confirmed.suggested_fix: Replace '@inertiajs/react 2+' with '@inertiajs/react 3+' (or '^3.0.0') across all six locations to match the actual peerDependencies and ADR-001.
confirmed.detail: Line 25 reads 'peerDependencies for React 19+ and @inertiajs/react 2+'. Every JS package's actual peerDependencies pin '@inertiajs/react': '^3.0.0' (verified in packages-js/react/package.json, packages-js/ui/package.json, packages-js/hooks/package.json). BUG-VAL-009 (docs/tickets/current.md) explicitly unified this away from '^2' to '^3.0.0', and ADR-001 makes Inertia 3 the only supported bridge. '2+' implies Inertia 2 is supported, which is false. Same stale '2+' appears in apps/docs/pt-BR/reference/typescript-overview.md:25, apps/docs/es/reference/typescript-overview.md:25, and in the per-package react reference at apps/docs/reference/typescript/react.md:12, apps/docs/pt-BR/reference/typescript/react.md:12, apps/docs/es/reference/typescript/react.md:12.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides on disk. Doc side: apps/docs/reference/typescript-overview.md:25 reads "peerDependencies for React 19+ and @inertiajs/react 2+"; apps/docs/reference/typescript/react.md:12 reads "peerDependencies: React 19+, @inertiajs/react 2+". Code side: grep of packages-js/{react,ui,hooks}/package.json shows every peerDependencies entry pins "@inertiajs/react": "^3.0.0" (react:64, ui:109, hooks:90/97). "^3.0.0" excludes all of Inertia 2, so "2+" (which asserts v2 is an acceptable peer) is objectively false/misleading. Reinforced by ADR-001 (Inertia 3 only) and BUG-VAL-009. Files are in-scope reference docs (not PLANNING/, not docs/tickets, not CHANGELOG, not generated). Finding not present in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json (no inertia/typescript-overview/typescript-react entries). The pt-BR and es mirrors share the same stale string per the finding's claim, but the EN files alone establish a real, checkable contradiction.

confirmed.title: nav.md documents a label(string) setter on NavigationItem and NavigationGroup that does not exist
confirmed.suggested_fix: Remove the `label(string)` setter rows from the NavigationItem and NavigationGroup tables (lines 11 and 31) in all three nav.md files, or note that the label is set via `make(string $label)`.
confirmed.detail: The NavigationItem method table (line 11) lists '| `label(string)` | `self` | |' and the NavigationGroup section (line 31) lists '`label(string)`, `icon(string)`, `sort(int)` | Base setters'. packages/nav/src/NavigationItem.php has no public function label() (label is set only via the static make(string $label) factory; only getLabel() exists). packages/nav/src/NavigationGroup.php likewise has icon() and sort() but no label() setter. The icon()/sort() entries are correct; only label() is fabricated. Mirrors carry the same error: apps/docs/pt-BR/reference/php/nav.md:11 and :31, apps/docs/es/reference/php/nav.md:11 and :31.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides. Doc apps/docs/reference/php/nav.md:11 lists `label(string)` -> `self` as a NavigationItem setter, and :31 lists `label(string)` among NavigationGroup "Base setters". Reading packages/nav/src/NavigationItem.php and NavigationGroup.php: each declares `protected string $label`, sets it only in `__construct` / `make(string $label)`, and exposes only `getLabel()` — there is NO public `label()` setter and no `__call`/magic fallback. The sibling `icon()` (NavigationItem:97 / Group:42) and `sort()` (Item:153 / Group:86) setters DO exist, so those table entries are correct; only `label()` is fabricated. Doc is in-scope (apps/docs reference, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated). No nav.md / label entry in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json, so not a duplicate. The claim is a verifiable factual contradiction, not a wording/missing-doc nit.
